name: backend-fundamentals
description: Auto-invoke when reviewing API routes, server logic, Express/Node.js code, or backend architecture. Enforces REST conventions, middleware patterns, and separation of concerns.
capabilities: Checks RESTful API conventions and HTTP methods
Validates proper error handling and status codes
Reviews separation of concerns between layers
Identifies security vulnerabilities in route handlers
Provides HTTP status code reference guide
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: This skill contains only documentation - markdown guidelines and JSON metadata. All static findings are false positives triggered by: (1) SHA-256 content hashes incorrectly flagged as C2 keywords, (2) Hash strings identified as 'weak cryptographic algorithms' (SHA-256 is secure), (3) Markdown backticks in code examples flagged as shell execution. No executable code, network calls, or file system access exists.
valueStatement: Backend code reviews often miss critical security and design issues. This skill automatically checks your Express routes, middleware, and API endpoints against industry best practices.
userTitle: Review backend code for API best practices
limitations: Requires manual review for business logic errors

useCases.title: Learn backend best practices
useCases.description: Get instant feedback on API design, error handling, and architecture patterns while learning backend development fundamentals.

useCases.title: Standardize code reviews
useCases.description: Ensure consistent backend code quality across your team with automated checks for REST conventions and security patterns.

useCases.title: Onboard new team members
useCases.description: Provide structured guidance for backend development standards and catch common mistakes before they reach production.

promptTemplates.title: Basic API Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review these Express routes for REST conventions, error handling, and separation of concerns: [paste your route code]

promptTemplates.title: Authentication Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if these API endpoints have proper authentication and authorization. Are sensitive routes protected? [paste your auth code]

promptTemplates.title: Error Handling Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Audit this backend code for error handling. Are errors properly caught, logged, and returned to clients? [paste your error handling code]

promptTemplates.title: Architecture Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this backend architecture for separation of concerns. Should business logic be moved to services? Are routes too fat? [describe your architecture]

bestPractices: Always validate input before processing requests
Use proper HTTP status codes for all responses
Keep routes thin - move logic to controllers/services
antiPatterns: Putting business logic directly in route handlers
Using verbs in API endpoint names (/getUsers)
Returning 200 OK with error messages in body

faq.question: Which backend frameworks does this support?
faq.answer: Primarily Express.js, but principles apply to Fastify, Hono, and other Node.js frameworks.

faq.question: Can it detect SQL injection vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It identifies patterns like string concatenation in queries and suggests parameterized queries.

faq.question: Does it work with non-Node.js backends?
faq.answer: REST principles are universal, but examples focus on JavaScript/TypeScript implementations.

faq.question: How does it handle file uploads?
faq.answer: Reviews validation, size limits, and security headers for multipart form data endpoints.

faq.question: Can it suggest pagination implementations?
faq.answer: Yes, it flags missing pagination on list endpoints and suggests cursor or offset patterns.

faq.question: Does it check for rate limiting?
faq.answer: It identifies endpoints that should have rate limiting based on their purpose and suggests implementation.
